enrichment.alert_summary: IAM user svc-deploy accessed production database credentials from an unusual IP address (203.0.113.45) at 3:42 AM UTC. This service account is not expected to access Secrets Manager directly, suggesting possible credential theft or unauthorized use of the deployment service account.
enrichment.business_impact: Production database credentials may be compromised, potentially enabling unauthorized access to all production data.
investigation_queries: SELECT * FROM cloudtrail_logs WHERE useridentity.username='svc-deploy' AND eventtime > NOW() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR ORDER BY eventtime DESC
fields @timestamp, sourceIPAddress, eventName | filter userIdentity.userName = 'svc-deploy' | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50
SELECT sourceipaddress, COUNT(*) as count FROM cloudtrail WHERE eventname='GetSecretValue' GROUP BY sourceipaddress ORDER BY count DESC
remediation_steps: Disable IAM user svc-deploy access keys and review attached policies
Review all API calls from 203.0.113.45 in the last 30 days
Enable MFA for all IAM users with Secrets Manager access
Add IP-based conditions to the IAM policy for svc-deploy
